name: BRONCHIAL ATRESIA
definition: rare anomaly resulting from focal obliteration of proximal lumen of a lobar / segmental /

demographics.Path: normal bronchial tree distal to occlusion is patent and filled with mucus; alveoli distal
demographics.Location: apicoposterior segment of LUL (>> RUL / ML)

dominant_finding: mucus-filled bronchus near hilum WITHOUT connection between mucocele + hilum
distribution: apicoposterior segment of LUL (>> RUL / ML)
discriminating_features: CHARACTERISTIC "gloved finger" sign ← accumulation of mucus in dilated central
mucus-filled bronchus near hilum WITHOUT connection between mucocele + hilum
decreased perfusion with focal parenchymal oligemia
large echogenic fetal lung mass ← lung fluid-filled distal to occlusion (indistinguishable

CXR: lobular / ovoid / round / branching perihilar mass:
CHARACTERISTIC "gloved finger" sign ← accumulation of mucus in dilated central
overexpanded hyperlucent lung segment (collateral air drift with expiratory air-trapping)
CT: mucus-filled bronchus near hilum WITHOUT connection between mucocele + hilum
decreased perfusion with focal parenchymal oligemia
large echogenic fetal lung mass ← lung fluid-filled distal to occlusion (indistinguishable
focal lung mass of homogeneously high SI on T2WI

clinical.Rx: no treatment in asymptomatic patients; segmentectomy in symptomatic infected patients
